evidence: Rapid advances in AI may also create competitive pressures or require ongoing investment to maintain effectiveness
Emerging and evolving regulations related to AI use... may require additional investment, changes to existing processes
Artificial intelligence (AI) technologies support a range of the Company s activities, including research and development, operational processes, customer facing tools, and other business functions.
changes in the business environment related to disruptive technologies, such as artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies
AI systems may produce inaccurate, biased, or incomplete outputs, which could lead to operational errors, reduced product or service quality, or unintentional impacts on business decisions.
Use of AI tools, including third party platforms, increases the risk of unauthorized disclosure, loss, or misuse of confidential, personal, or commercially sensitive information.
failures to manage, govern, or deploy AI responsibly could impair the Company s ability to achieve expected efficiencies or strategic objectives

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-attributable spend, headcount, or named AI programs disclosed in the excerpts; investment references are forward-looking and conditional rather than describing committed resources.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Named use-case categories (R&D, operational processes, customer-facing tools) are listed but no deployment status, metrics, or specific product names are provided; scored 3 rather than 4.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and contains detailed AI risk scenarios with named mechanisms (data breach, regulatory compliance, vendor opacity, governance failure) but no quantification or named oversight bodies, placing it at 4 rather than 5.
